**Clinical Reasoning:**
Given the presence of intracranial calcification seen on CT scans, together with the skin manifestation and the family history where both the father and the young son share similar skin issues indicative of ash-leaf macule patterns consistent with Tuberous Sclerosis Complex (TSC), the most probable diagnosis aligns closely with TSC.
